snippets.title: Basic t-SNE with sklearn (2D visualization)
snippets.code:
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.datasets import load_digits

X, y = load_digits(return_X_y=True)
X_scaled = StandardScaler().fit_transform(X)

tsne = TSNE(
    n_components=2,
    perplexity=30,
    learning_rate="auto",
    init="pca",
    random_state=0,
    n_iter=1000,
)
Y = tsne.fit_transform(X_scaled)

print("embedding shape:", Y.shape)  # (n_samples, 2)
snippets.explanation: `fit_transform` learns affinities and returns 2D coordinates for visualization only; `init="pca"` gives a stable starting layout (see PCA topic for what PCA does).

snippets.title: PCA preprocess then t-SNE (common speed trick)
snippets.code:
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("pca", PCA(n_components=50, random_state=0)),
    ("tsne", TSNE(n_components=2, perplexity=30, random_state=0)),
])

Y = pipe.fit_transform(X_scaled)
print("steps:", [s[0] for s in pipe.steps])
snippets.explanation: Reducing to 50 PCA components before t-SNE speeds up O(n²) affinity work without re-deriving PCA math here.

snippets.title: Compare perplexity values
snippets.code:
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

for perp in (5, 30, 50):
    Y = TSNE(
        n_components=2,
        perplexity=perp,
        random_state=0,
        init="pca",
    ).fit_transform(X_scaled)
    plt.scatter(Y[:, 0], Y[:, 1], s=8, alpha=0.7, label=f"perp={perp}")

plt.legend()
plt.title("t-SNE perplexity comparison")
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: Plot several perplexities side by side; choose the scale that best reveals the structure you care about without fragmenting into noise.

snippets.title: Color by label for EDA (labels not used in fit)
snippets.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE

Y = TSNE(n_components=2, perplexity=30, random_state=0).fit_transform(X_scaled)

plt.scatter(Y[:, 0], Y[:, 1], c=y, cmap="tab10", s=10, alpha=0.8)
plt.colorbar(label="class")
plt.xlabel("t-SNE 1")
plt.ylabel("t-SNE 2")
plt.title("Unsupervised layout, supervised coloring")
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: t-SNE is unsupervised; coloring by y checks whether local neighborhoods align with known groups—do not pass labels into fit.
